copy.subject: A question about your kid
copy.preview: - **Preview:** 10 seconds, helps us send you better tips. No wrong answer.

sections.name: Hook
sections.content:
It's been two weeks since your order arrived. How's it going so far?

Maybe your kid is starting to pause when they feel the wetness. Maybe they haven't noticed at all yet. Maybe you're somewhere in between, a look here, a tug at the pants there, nothing consistent. All of that is completely normal for this stage. 

Every kid's nervous system works on its own timeline. Some start showing awareness after two weeks. Others need eight. Both are normal. The only thing that matters is that the signal is getting a chance to arrive, and now we know it is, because the underwear is creating the feedback loop the brain needs.

sections.name: Validation
sections.content:
We've been doing this long enough to know: every family's situation is different. Your child might be 3 or 10. Verbal or non-verbal. Diagnosed or still figuring it out. You might have tried one method or twelve. You might be feeling hopeful or you might be protecting yourself from disappointment. 

Wherever you are on that spectrum, you're not alone in it. Two weeks into the Body-Signal approach, the most common experience is "nothing dramatic happened yet." That's not failure. That's the nervous system doing exactly what it needs to do, adjusting to receiving a signal it's never had before.

sections.name: Mechanism
sections.content:
Here's what we know happens next. The 30-60 seconds of gentle "uh-oh" sensation after every accident gives the brainformation it's been missing. Each time that sensation arrives, a tiny neural connection strengthens. One day, sometimes two weeks in, sometimes eight, the brain connects the dots: "This feeling means I peed. I should use the potty." 

The first sign is almost never "I need to go." It's smaller. A pause. A look down. A hand touching the underwear. A step toward the bathroom, even after it's already happened. That small moment is the entire mechanism working. 

### Section 4: Help Us Help You (The Detection Moment)
To make sure the emails you get from here match your child's experience, could you answer one question? It takes ten seconds. There's no wrong answer.

Which of these sounds most like your child right now?

⬜ Talks about the potty but won't actually do it

⬜ Sensory issues, tried many methods, still stuck

⬜ Non-verbal or high support needs, progress looks different here

⬜ Still figuring out what's going on, keep it general

sections.name: Guarantee / Risk Reversal
sections.content: Pick whichever one fits. There's no wrong choice, and whatever you tell us, the 60-day guarantee still stands. You decide if this approach is working for your family.

sections.name: CTA
sections.content:
- Button text: (The selected button text, linked to the appropriate action)
- Placement: Section 4, the detection moment IS the CTA. No additional CTA needed after the buttons.

signoff.text:
With understanding,
Lena ——
Customer Support · Mom of two autistic sons

P.S. Both my sons went through this. One took 6, the other almost 8. However long it takes, we're here.

footer.text:
BrightKidCo
[Physical Address]
[Unsubscribe link]

Reply to this email, Lena reads every response.

- Tonality calibration: Layer-17-Tonality-Calibration.md §4 (Stage 4, Post-Purchase Early)
- Avatar levels: GIULIANO-DEMANDS.md §1, Level marker phrases for all 4 levels
- Sub-groups: Layer-21-Sub-Groups.md, Sub-Groups A-E identity states, "Still figuring it out" language
- Mechanism: Synthesis 04-Mechanism-Positioning.md §4.1, Body-Signal Learning Layer, 30-60 second feedback window
- Brand voice: Synthesis 11-Brand-Voice-Reference.md §9.4, MEDIUM sign-off
- Button mapping: S2 §1.9, 4 buttons cleanly map to avatar_level1/2/3/general tags
- Product context: GIULIANO-DEMANDS.md §2.2, PP-Level Detection fires Day 14 for GF purchasers only
